puzzle0.title: CREW MANIFEST CORRELATION
puzzle0.description: Match each recovered record with the correct anomaly.

event0.text: If we're going to survive, you need to know who we were.

event3.text: At least, that's what the official manifest says.

event5.text: Names are what the station keeps after it has already failed the people attached to them.

event1.text: ECHO wasn't run by one person, no matter how much Hale liked that idea.

event10.text: He would deny that, by the way.

event12.text: Then he would explain exactly why everyone else was technically wrong.

event19.text: I used to trust official things.

event1.text: Hale pretended he didn't need anyone.

event3.text: Rivera solved problems before anyone finished explaining them.

event5.text: That sounds useful until you're halfway through a sentence and she has already fixed your mistake and judged your childhood.

event7.text: Park made jokes when he was scared. Owens pretended not to laugh.

event2.text: He was the worst liar of all of us.

event16.text: Grant treated every maintenance request like a security threat.

event18.text: Owens called him paranoid. Grant called that 'evidence of poor threat assessment.'

event20.text: We laughed at him. Mostly because he was usually right.

event26.text: It erased people who had annoying habits. Bad jokes. Favorite mugs. Arguments that never got finished.

event0.title: CREW MANIFEST
event0.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
event0.caption: Recovered official ECHO crew manifest.

event0.text: Grant handled security. Head of Not Dying, he called himself.

event3.text: He said it as a joke, but Grant had this way of joking like he was writing his own job description.

event1.text: Rivera managed logistics. Park and Owens were stuck in the lower sectors.

event2.text: Nobody liked the lower sectors. Park pretended he did. Owens called him a liar every morning.

event9.text: Park once said the lower sectors had 'character.'

event11.text: Owens said mold also has character.

event18.text: People were like that on ECHO.

choice0.text: WHO DO YOU MISS THE MOST?

event1.text: Power, relay routing, pressure control, emergency doors. If something opened or closed, Hale probably had a way into it.

event5.text: Ethan could open almost any door on the station.

event7.text: The strange thing is that he was terrible at opening up to people.

event12.text: Digital reports. Red comments. On a station.

event14.text: I told him that was hostile.

event16.text: He said accuracy was not a friendship contest.

event2.text: That's why I don't like hearing his voice in places it shouldn't be.

event21.text: Because the real Hale was hard to like sometimes.

event1.title: LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION
event1.content:
ECHO LIFE SUPPORT SNAPSHOT

Assigned crew capacity: 6
Active biometric loads before blackout: 7
Oxygen adjustment event: automatic
Unauthorized load marker: removed from index
event1.caption: Recovered life support allocation record. Crew count mismatch detected.
event1.message: [LIFE SUPPORT ALLOCATION ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.title: SUIT REGISTRY
event2.content:
ECHO EVA SUIT REGISTRY

SUIT-01: ETHAN HALE
SUIT-02: MAYA CHEN
SUIT-03: GRANT WALKER
SUIT-04: RIVERA TORRES
SUIT-05: OWEN PARK
SUIT-06: SARAH OWENS
SUIT-07: ENTRY ERASED

Registry repair failed.
event2.caption: Recovered EVA suit registry. Seventh suit slot exists but identity is erased.

event16.text: That's the kind of sentence people say when they want to feel safe and technical.

event18.text: But life support doesn't care about names.

event23.text: And the suits counted one more than we did.

event0.text: No photo. No face. No neat little proof you can stare at until it makes sense.

event1.text: Just systems disagreeing with each other. That's worse, honestly.

event2.text: Systems don't panic. They don't hallucinate. They just record the wrong truth.

event8.text: I almost wish we'd found a body.

event10.text: Bodies are simple. Records are not.

event12.text: A body says: someone was here.

event14.text: An erased record says: someone decided they shouldn't have been.

event19.text: And this station was already cold enough.

event1.text: And I know how convenient that sounds.

event2.text: But if there was a seventh person on ECHO, someone worked very hard to make us forget them.

event12.text: I don't remember another ration allocation.

event14.text: But now that I know there should be a gap...

event4.text: Six names. Seven biometric loads. One erased suit.

event6.text: You're looking for the hole, not the people around it.

event10.text: If we rush this, we become the archive. Clean labels. Wrong story.

event2.title: MANIFEST_V2
event2.content:
01 Ethan Hale
02 Maya Chen
03 Grant Walker
04 Rivera Torres
05 Owen Park
06 Sarah Owens
07 █████████

Layer status: identity erased, not redacted.
event2.caption: Recovered alternate manifest layer. Seventh entry erased.

event10.text: Six people allowed to remain people.

event12.text: One turned into a wound in the file.

event0.text: I don't know who Number 7 is.

event1.text: One whisper from the dark and suddenly I'm the suspect.

event9.text: When you're scared, a person is easier to blame than a hole in reality.

event2.text: Fine. Keep that question. Just don't let it stop you from asking the right one.

event14.text: Because whoever erased Number 7 did not stop at a name.

event3.text: ETHAN HALE WAS NOT THE FIRST VOICE.

event12.text: Maya doesn't answer for a moment.

event14.text: When she does, her signal is quieter.

event18.text: Ask the archive what spoke before Hale.

choice1.text: ASK MAYA ABOUT THE FIRST VOICE.

event3.text: Life support counted seven biometric loads before blackout.

event5.text: Suit registry has a seventh slot, but the name is erased.

event8.text: Not redacted. Not hidden behind clearance.

event6.text: Because my first answer changed three times before I could type.

event8.text: I miss Rivera when something breaks.

event10.text: I miss Grant when something moves in the dark.

event12.text: I miss Park when the silence gets too big.

event14.text: I miss Owens when I need someone to tell me I'm being stupid and still hand me coffee.

event19.text: I miss being angry at Hale.
